value.Title: Skyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-95G3-SFDD-V19G-00000-00Skyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:41ZLexisNexisSkyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio

Renewable energy investor Skyview Ventures and its solar firm Davis Hill Development has expanded its partnership with HiveTracks to integrate apiaries and environmental monitoring across its solar portfolio.

The companys current solar offerings span multiple states, including Maryland, Connecticut, and New York, plus Washington, D.C. Using HiveTracks biodiversity intelligence platform will allow the company to scale data-driven environmental monitoring and managed pollinator programs across several solar projects, incorporating pollinator habitats into existing solar projects.

This partnership reflects our broader vision that renewable energy projects should be developed with long-term environmental stewardship in mind, says Andy Karetsky, CEO of Skyview Ventures. By integrating biodiversity monitoring, pollinator habitats, and data-driven land management into our solar portfolio, we are demonstrating that solar projects can deliver environmental benefits beyond clean energy generation and play a meaningful role in supporting local ecosystems.

Skyview and HiveTracks have been working together since 2025, the companies say, beginning their collaboration with eight solar projects across New York, West Virginia, Tennessee, and Connecticut. Thanks to the partnerships initial success, the two firms have planned expansion to additional Davis Hill sites throughout 2026.

Local beekeepers will tend to each solar site throughout the lifespan of its given apiary, the company says. Beyond the production of bottles of honey which in 2025 was given away to food banks the apiaries will serve as environmental indicators, giving the companies numbers on ecosystem health and land use impact over time.

solar apiary

Apiary analysis

As part of the agreement, HiveTrack will collect environmental metrics for the solar sites, both on a per-site and portfolio-wide level, company officials say. The metrics will give insight to pollinator and plant diversity and abundance, as well as testing for eDNA and ecotoxicology in each hive.

Solar energy already provides environmental benefits, but with HiveTracks, we saw an opportunity to go further, says Maria Morales Ferrebus, solar development associate at Skyview Ventures. In our first year, the data provided valuable insights into ecosystem health across our sites. For example, we identified areas with low plant diversity, which can reduce resilience to drought conditions and increase erosion risk.

By implementing HiveTracks recommendations, we can adapt our operations and maintenance practices to enhance plant diversity, improve soil health, and strengthen pollinator habitats.

The apiary program currently spans multiple projects throughout varying stages of development, the company says, with some affected projects already fully operational. This approach allows Skyview to monitor environmental impacts of solar projects through each stage of development, representatives say, with HiveTracks providing site-specific recommendations.

The heart of the program reflects a wider trend around the solar industry, Skyview says, where developers now have an outsized focus on multi-use land strategies. As part of the shift, solar projects have become synonymous with farming, habitat restoration, and biodiversity initiatives around the U.S. and beyond.

Solar sites represent a unique opportunity to support pollinators and monitor biodiversity at scale, says Max Rnzel, CEO of HiveTracks. Our partnership with Skyview Ventures demonstrates how beekeeping, environmental data, and renewable energy infrastructure can work together to create measurable environmental impact.

value.Title: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-FJP3-S0BP-Y0B7-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHH-T8D3-S949-V3MK-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-G413-RT9J-2079-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH7-03C3-RS0T-82Y2-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Jurisdiction: Missouri; Washington
value.Title: Plans for solar farms raise concerns, calls for action

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-BR33-RV78-62J1-00000-00Plans for solar farms raise concerns, calls for action2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisCITY & COUNTYPlans for solar farms raise concerns, calls for actionChris Howell chris.howell@sedaliademocrat.com

As solar energy development expands across rural Missouri, concerns are growing among residents in and around Pettis County about the pace of projects and the lack of statewide regulation.

Megan McKenzie-Santiago, who lives near Pilot Grove and grew up in Cooper County, says her concerns aren't about opposing renewable energy outright but about what she sees as a regulatory gap.

"People have a right to do what they want with their property," McKenzie-Santiago said. "Property rights are a big thing to farmers and landowners. But what I've found in my research is that Missouri has no legislation on the books for green energy installation and how it should be handled."

That absence of statewide rules, she says, leaves both landowners and neighboring properties in uncertain territory.

"Our concern is that there are no regulations these companies have to follow as they come into Missouri," McKenzie-Santiago said. "And in rural counties, there's generally no zoning either."

While many rural residents oppose heavy zoning restrictions, McKenzie-Santiago said there is still a need for environmental safeguards and consideration of neighboring properties.

"You have to ask what rights do neighboring landowners have if these developments start affecting their land?" McKenzie-Santiago said. "There are concerns about erosion, groundwater, and even potential contamination if panels are damaged by storms."

Those concerns have been amplified by recent severe weather across central Missouri.

"There's always the possibility of tornadoes or hail," McKenzie-Santiago said. "What happens if these panels are damaged? What's released into the environment?"

McKenzie-Santiago said interest in the issue intensified after reports of surveyors and drones operating near Pilot Grove private property earlier this spring.

"That was witnessed by multiple landowners," McKenzie-Santiago said. "There were rumors before, but people didn't want to go off rumors. Then they started seeing activity."

That activity led to a series of local meetings, including a community gathering attended by county commissioners and state officials. Among them was State Rep. Tim Taylor, as well as discussions referencing legislative efforts by Rep. Brad Pollitt.

At the state level, attention has also turned to a proposal from Sen. Cindy O'Laughlin, who has advanced SB 849, a bill that could temporarily halt new solar developments.

"The idea of a moratorium is a step in the right direction," McKenzie-Santiago said. "It would give the state time to actually put regulations in place and understand the long-term impacts."

She emphasized that the issue goes beyond solar panels alone, pointing to broader concerns about energy infrastructure and emerging technologies.

"If you look at what's happening in other states, there are connections between large-scale energy production and things like AI data centers," McKenzie-Santiago said. "Those facilities require huge amounts of power and water, and that raises additional concerns for rural communities."

Missouri's agricultural identity also plays a role in the debate, particularly in counties like Pettis and Cooper.

"This is a major ag region," McKenzie-Santiago said. "We're home to the Missouri State Fair. Agriculture is a huge part of our economy, and people want to make sure that's protected."

For residents trying to make sense of the issue, McKenzie-Santiago encourages independent research and civic engagement.

"When I started looking into this, most of the information online was from solar companies themselves," McKenzie-Santiago said. "People need to ask questions, where is the funding coming from, who is manufacturing these panels, and what happens at the end of their lifespan?"

She also pointed to policies in other states, such as Washington, which requires companies to have end-of-life plans for solar panels, including buyback or recycling programs.

"That's something Missouri doesn't have right now," McKenzie-Santiagoe said. "And without it, there are still a lot of unanswered questions."

At the local level, she noted that county commissions may have limited, but important authority.

"Even without state legislation, there are some statutes that allow county commissioners to slow things down," McKenzie-Santiago said. "They do have some power, and that's something people should be aware of."

As discussions continue, McKenzie-Santiago said her goal is not to stop development entirely, but to ensure it is done thoughtfully.

"This isn't about killing green energy," McKenzie-Santiago said. "It's about making sure we understand it, regulate it properly, and protect the people and land that are already here."

Rep. Brad Pollitt said he had heard of the solar farm planned 30 miles from Sedalia and is rushing to push through a bill to offer some statewide regulation of the solar industry in Missouri.

"Right now we don't have any regulations and that's what we're working on with this bill,trying to just put some regulations out there for the state," Pollitt said. "Not trying to stop them, you know, it's a property rights issue but just something for the people who are around it to know what the state regulations are."

Without statewide regulations, solar farms could be headed to the area leaving little recourse for neighboring farmers when disputes arise.

If the bill does pass, county commissioners could end up being the last line of defense.

"It says in the bill that if a county wanted to do a Chapter 100, then they could change those regulations," Pollitt said. "But we would just give them a skeleton framework on if you want to just go with the state regulations here it is; it talks about setbacks and taxation and those types of things but you know we're still working on it, two weeks left in the session and there's obviously some opposition to the bill or would have been moving a little better."

For more information visit the Pilot Grove - Solar Field Project Conversations and Concerns Facebook page, bit.ly/4d2zssB.

Chris Howell can be reached at 660-530-0146

Potential solar farms across the region are a topic of discussion, including in the Pilot Grove community in Cooper County. In this file photo, Farmland is seen with solar panels from Cypress Creek Renewables in Thurmont, Md. Photo by Joshua A. Bickel | AP Photo

urn:contentItem:6JH6-GB63-S0HJ-C05B-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JGY-P943-RVSH-W08F-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

urn:contentItem:6JH1-0XR3-RRT2-X135-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGW-WSB3-SBY9-J0RN-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: April 27, 2026: Clark Construction Group Welcomes Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources, National Campus Recruiting

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGH-RVD3-RS9K-G528-00000-00April 27, 2026: Clark Construction Group Welcomes Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources, National Campus Recruiting2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:28ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 27, 2026: Clark Construction Group Welcomes Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources, National Campus Recruiting

Carly Reiner Caceres has joined Clark Construction Group as Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting, effective Apr. 29, 2026. In this capacity, she will lead the company’s national campus recruiting program, shaping how Clark engages with universities to source early-career talent.

Caceres brings a track record of building HR strategy and operations in hybrid environments, most recently as Vice President of People Operations at Proper. Her background also includes leadership posts at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead, along with campus recruiting work at Grant Thornton LLP.

Her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding will support Clark Construction’s aim to recruit top graduates from across the country and strengthen internship programs and student engagement efforts.

Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has a long-standing reputation in education and infrastructure markets and emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Her early career in communications and external relations—spanning internships at Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum—rounds out a diverse background that informs her talent development strategies.

INDEX

SECTION 1 CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC PROFILE

SECTION 2 CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

urn:contentItem:6JGS-J3V3-S6FN-X37B-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY BRIEF FROM PEERS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY BRIEF FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGN-S093-S3VB-54S8-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: April 27, 2026: Carly Reiner Caceres Takes Helm of HR for National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGG-XW83-RTDV-C44W-00000-00April 27, 2026: Carly Reiner Caceres Takes Helm of HR for National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:28ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 27, 2026: Carly Reiner Caceres Takes Helm of HR for National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction

Carly Reiner Caceres has assumed the role of Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, effective Apr. 29, 2026, tasked with coordinating nationwide campus initiatives to expand early-career talent pipelines for the company.

Her career blends leadership in People Operations at Proper, where she guided HR strategy in a hybrid environment, with roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and nearly a decade at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.

Caceres’ experience spans full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder engagement, and employer branding, capabilities Clark Construction aims to leverage to attract top talent from universities across the United States. She has also led campus recruiting efforts at Grant Thornton LLP, strengthening internship programs and student engagement.

Clark Construction, founded in 1906, emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Caceres’ background in communications and external relations from early roles at Charleston Magazine and the Hirshhorn Museum is expected to support her work in nurturing the next generation of construction professionals.

INDEX

SECTION 1 CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC PROFILE

SECTION 2 CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

value.Title: April 22, 2026: Baltimore Event Highlights Workforce Pathways, Clean-Energy Opportunities and Fellowships

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-SPG3-RSND-R3YJ-00000-00April 22, 2026: Baltimore Event Highlights Workforce Pathways, Clean-Energy Opportunities and Fellowships2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 22, 2026: Baltimore Event Highlights Workforce Pathways, Clean-Energy Opportunities and Fellowships

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, gathering more than 250 leaders to discuss how to revitalize the economy and broaden opportunity.

The centerpiece is a three-year, $100 million program to connect workers with good jobs in 250 distressed communities, aiming to create about 1.6 million jobs across growth sectors including healthcare, energy transition, food systems and AI.

In addition, the Foundation released a $12 million boost for Invest in Our Future to advance clean energy opportunities and growth in underserved areas, while Maryland Governor Wes Moore announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards for education and childcare in high-poverty neighborhoods. The event also unveiled the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows and underscored collaborations with the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next Baltimore-related event is scheduled for Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

INDEX

SECTION 1 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION PROFILE

SECTION 2 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.
